heading: Using Public IP Addresses
body:
You can use public IP addresses that refer to other internal or private
IP addresses using Network Address Translation to allow Atlas
traffic to your LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) server. If you do this, be aware that
certain activities trigger a change
in the Atlas cluster's public IP addresses.

If you allowed LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) server access based on public IP addresses,
changes to the Atlas cluster's public IP address prevent LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol)
access. To restore LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) access, add the new Atlas cluster public
IP addresses to the LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) access list.
